seed_problem: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: replay the online spot-vs-on-demand-vs-pause decision as a static full-trace schedule, and generalize from a single job to K deadline-windowed corridor segments sharing a scarce free-volunteer (spot) pool while a per-night contract mobilization fixed charge (on-demand dispatch) couples segments -> the offline optimum becomes a fixed-charge covering problem (NP-hard) instead of a poly DP.
title: Wildlife Corridor Night Watch: Offline Guard-Schedule Replay
strategies: trivial baseline: contract-only on the earliest W_i nights of each window (no volunteers, no clustering)
greedy: per-segment grab free volunteers then fill with cheapest per-guard-price contract nights, ignoring mobilization sharing

marginal-cost clustering: commit each guard at least marginal cost, amortizing the fixed mobilization fee across segments that share a contract night and spending scarce volunteers on the dearest nights
randomized multi-restart local search over segment processing order, keeping the cheapest schedule found
